lesson: Product bleeding out with the machine idle and a drain valve held open by crystallised syrup is a stuck valve. Trace the leak before blaming the float or gasket.
symptoms: Product level drops on its own even when not serving.
MIX LOW alarm going off repeatedly.

tests.error-log: Repeated MIX LOW alarms at irregular intervals.
tests.inspect-beater: Drain valve at the base of the bowl is held open by a fragment of crystallised syrup. Product slowly bleeding out.

symptoms: Sticky trail from the machine to the floor drain.

tests.inspect-beater: Drain valve seat wedged open by a shard of crystallised syrup. Slow bleed, all night, every night.

symptoms: MIX LOW alarms at random hours.
Cleaner mentions mopping syrup by the machine every morning.

tests.error-log: MIX LOW events at closed-hours timestamps. The machine loses product while nobody's serving.
tests.inspect-beater: A chip of old crystallised product holding the drain valve off its seat.

flavour: The machine wasn't losing its mind. Just its product.
authenticityNote: Drain valves can be held open by a shard of crystallised old product lodged in the seat, causing a slow seep that looks like a float switch or gasket issue until you trace the leak to the valve.
